devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एवं ब्रुवाणं मैत्रेयं द्वैपायनसुतो बुधः । प्रीणयन्निव भारत्या विदुरः प्रत्यभाषत ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca evaṃ bruvāṇaṃ maitreyaṃ dvaipāyanasuto budhaḥ prīṇayanniva bhāratyā viduraḥ pratyabhāṣata

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
To Maitreya who was speaking thus, Vidura, the learned son of Vyāsa, addressed (the following), as if to please him with his request.

devanagari: विदुर उवाच । ब्रह्मन् कथं भगवतः चिन्मात्रस्याविकारिणः । लीलया चापि युज्येरन् निर्गुणस्य गुणाः क्रियाः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: vidura uvāca brahman kathaṃ bhagavataḥ cinmātrasyāvikāriṇaḥ līlayā cāpi yujyeran nirguṇasya guṇāḥ kriyāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Vidura said:
Oh Brahman! How is it possible that attributes (like sattva, rajas) and activity can be predicated of the Supreme Lord who is pure consciousness, devoid of attributes and changeless, even by way of sport (the līlā-theory)?

devanagari: क्रीडायां उद्यमोऽर्भस्य कामश्चिक्रीडिषान्यतः । स्वतस्तृप्तस्य च कथं निवृत्तस्य सदान्यतः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: krīḍāyāṃ udyamo svatastṛptasya ca kathaṃ nivṛttasya sadānyataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [genitive single from √ tṛp class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ tṛp class 4 verb], [genitive single from √ tṛp class 5 verb], [genitive single from √ tṛp class 6 verb]
[genitive single from √ tṛp class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ tṛp class 4 verb], [genitive single from √ tṛp class 5 verb], [genitive single from √ tṛp class 6 verb]

translation: It is the desire in the case of a child which propels it to play, and that desire to play comes from something else (e.g. a toy or invitation to play), but how (is it applicable to God) who is self-satisfied and who is ever without a second.

devanagari: अस्राक्षीत् भगवान् विश्वं गुणमय्याऽत्ममायया । तया संस्थापयत्येतद् भूयः प्रत्यपिधास्यति ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: asrākṣīt bhagavān viśvaṃ guṇamayyā tayā saṃsthāpayatyetad bhūyaḥ pratyapidhāsyati

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ āp ], [vocative dual from √ āp ], [accusative dual from √ āp ], [locative single from √ āp ]
[nominative dual from √ i ], [vocative dual from √ i ], [accusative dual from √ i ], [locative single from √ i ]

translation: (It is argued that) the Supreme Lord created the universe by his Māyā consisting of guṇas and (which deludes the Soul to feel that he is the doer and enjoyer). It is by Māyā that he protects it and withdraws it. The Soul is essentially of the nature of knowledge or consciousness and unaffected by place, time or condition either internally (through himself) or externally (through other agency). How can such Soul be united with the unborn (Māyā)?

devanagari: देशतः कालतो योऽसौ अवस्थातः स्वतोऽन्यतः । अविलुप्तावबोधात्मा स युज्येताजया कथम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: deśataḥ kālato yo aviluptāvabodhātmā sa yujyetājayā katham
translation: (It is argued that) the Supreme Lord created the universe by his Māyā consisting of guṇas and (which deludes the Soul to feel that he is the doer and enjoyer). It is by Māyā that he protects it and withdraws it. The Soul is essentially of the nature of knowledge or consciousness and unaffected by place, time or condition either internally (through himself) or externally (through other agency). How can such Soul be united with the unborn (Māyā)?

devanagari: भगवानेक एवैष सर्वक्षेत्रेष्ववस्थितः । अमुष्य दुर्भगत्वं वा क्लेशो वा कर्मभिः कुतः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavāneka evaiṣa sarvakṣetreṣvavasthitaḥ amuṣya durbhagatvaṃ vā kleśo vā karmabhiḥ kutaḥ
translation: Only this Supreme Lord alone is present in all bodies. How can he be subjected to misfortune or pain by karmas? (i.e. saṃsāra does not exist in the case of jīva as he is the same as Brahman).

devanagari: एतस्मिन्मे मनो विद्वन् खिद्यतेऽज्ञानसङ्कटे । तन्नः पराणुद विभो कश्मलं मानसं महत् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: etasminme mano vidvan khidyate tannaḥ parāṇuda vibho kaśmalaṃ mānasaṃ mahat
translation: Oh learned one! My mind feels depressed in this calamity of ignorance. Oh Lord! Please remove this great mental delusion from our mind.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । स इत्थं चोदितः क्षत्त्रा तत्त्वजिज्ञासुना मुनिः । प्रत्याह भगवच्चित्तः स्मयन्निव गतस्मयः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca sa itthaṃ coditaḥ kṣattrā tattvajijñāsunā muniḥ pratyāha bhagavaccittaḥ smayanniva gatasmayaḥ

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
The sage who was thus prompted by Vidura who was desirous of knowing the truth, smilingly replied, with his mind concentrated on the Lord, and free from pride.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । सेयं भगवतो माया यन्नयेन विरुध्यते । ईश्वरस्य विमुक्तस्य कार्पण्यमुत बन्धनम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca seyaṃ bhagavato māyā yannayena virudhyate īśvarasya vimuktasya kārpaṇyamuta bandhanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

[nominative single from √ si class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ si class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ si class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ si class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: It is the Māyā of the Almighty Lord that is against all logic (supra-logical). Hence (the experience of) affliction and bondage to (the soul who is essentially) free from bondage (and misery).[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Kramasandarbha. in his long com. raises the point why the Soul (jīva) who is a form of God suffers the bondage of affliction, and explains that it is due to the supra-logical nature of the māyā-śakti of God. The power of God is both internal (antaraṅga) and external (bahiraṅga). Hence what happens in the region of the external of God cannot affect his own internal nature. Thus though God in the form of jīvas may experience bondage or suffering, he is internally unaffected. It is this supra-logical conception which explains how God can be within the power of the Māyā as well as its controller.

devanagari: यदर्थेन विनामुष्य पुंस आत्मविपर्ययः । प्रतीयत उपद्रष्टुः स्वशिरश्छेदनादिकः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: yadarthena vināmuṣya puṃsa ātmaviparyayaḥ pratīyata upadraṣṭuḥ svaśiraśchedanādikaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Just as a man witnessing a dream gets an inconsistent experience of being beheaded etc., though it is not a reality.[2]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Just as in the case of jīva, in the stage when he has not realised the Soul (ātman), he wrongly identifies himself with the boḍy which is in reality different from him, and experiences beheading etc. But after self-realization, knowing his separateness from the body, and his being a part of God, he is not thus affected.—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: यथा जले चन्द्रमसः कम्पादिस्तत्कृतो गुणः । दृश्यतेऽसन्नपि द्रष्टुः आत्मनोऽनात्मनो गुणः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā jale candramasaḥ kampādistatkṛto guṇaḥ dṛśyate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: Just as trembling and other disturbances caused by the water appear to be the attribute of the moon (reflected) in the water, even though it is not attributable to the (real) Moon (in the sky), similarly attributes of the non-Soul (the physical body etc.) seem to belong to the Soul (jīva), a seer (who identifies himself with the body).[3]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā points to the distinction between the Soul (ātmā) or Jīva and Supreme Soul (paramātmā). Sorrow, delusion etc. are caused, as jīva mistakenly thinks himself to be the boḍy. The Supreme Soul who is not influenced (lit. covered) by Avidyā and who is the controller of Prakṛti and Puruṣa is unaffected by the effects or working of avidyā.

devanagari: स वै निवृत्तिधर्मेण वासुदेवानुकम्पया । भगवद्‍भक्तियोगेन तिरोधत्ते शनैरिह ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vai nivṛttidharmeṇa vāsudevānukampayā bhagavad‍bhaktiyogena tirodhatte śanairiha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Verily it is by the path of nivṛtti (abstinence from work—by giving up desire for its fruit), through the grace of Vāsudeva, and by devotion to the Almighty Lord, that this (mistaken identification of the Soul with the body) slowly disappears here.

devanagari: यदेन्द्रियोपरामोऽथ द्रष्ट्रात्मनि परे हरौ । विलीयन्ते तदा क्लेशाः संसुप्तस्येव कृत्स्नशः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: yadendriyoparāmo vilīyante tadā kleśāḥ saṃsuptasyeva kṛtsnaśaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle third plural], [present passive third plural]

translation: Now, when the organs of the senses (instead of pursuing outer objects) become steadfast on Hari, the Seer, the internal controller (antaryāmin), the Supreme Lord, all the afflictions completely subside (disappear) as in the case of a man who is fast asleep.

devanagari: अशेषसङ्क्लेशशमं विधत्ते । गुणानुवादश्रवणं मुरारेः । किं वा पुनस्तच्चरणारविन्द । परागसेवारतिरात्मलब्धा ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: aśeṣasaṅkleśaśamaṃ vidhatte guṇānuvādaśravaṇaṃ murāreḥ kiṃ vā punastaccaraṇāravinda parāgasevāratirātmalabdhā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vidh class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ vidh class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ vidh class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ vidh class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ vidh class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ vidh class 6 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Repetition (of the description of) and listening to the attributes of Hari, cause the complete subsidence (disappearance) of miseries. What again (need be spoken of the effect of) the love felt in the heart, for worshipping the pollendust of His lotus-like feet.

devanagari: विदुर उवाच । सञ्छिन्नः संशयो मह्यं तव सूक्तासिना विभो । उभयत्रापि भगवन् मनो मे सम्प्रधावति ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: vidura uvāca sañchinnaḥ saṃśayo mahyaṃ tava sūktāsinā vibho ubhayatrāpi bhagavan mano me sampradhāvati

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[accusative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ mah ]
[nominative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [nominative single from √ mah ], [accusative single from √ mah ]

translation:
Vidura said:
Oh Lord I my doubt has been cut off by the sword of your happy exposition. My mind can properly grasp both the absolute independence of the Supreme Lord and the dependence of jīva on him[4].
purport:
Footnote [4]:
ubhayatra... sampradhāvati: (1) Or: My mind can grasp the concepts of bondage and liberation—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā
(2) My mind is attracted both to the listening and repetition of his attributes as well as to the service of his feet—Bhāgavata Candrikā
(3) By ‘both’ Padaratnāvalī takes (i) attachment to the body and its belonging and (ii) God, Hari.
(4) Kramasandarbha. understands (i) the Supreme Lord and (ii) jīva or (i) knowledge and (ii) devotion which is a means to it.
(5) Subodhinī follows Bhāvāratha Dīpikā above.

devanagari: साध्वेतद् व्याहृतं विद्वन् आत्ममायायनं हरेः । आभात्यपार्थं निर्मूलं विश्वमूलं न यद्‍बहिः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: sādhvetad vyāhṛtaṃ vidvan ātmamāyāyanaṃ hareḥ ābhātyapārthaṃ nirmūlaṃ viśvamūlaṃ na yad‍bahiḥ
translation: [5] Oh learned one, you have well explained that all these unreal, rootless, miseries appear due to the basis of the ātma-māyā (māyā deluding the Soul or jīva). The root-cause of the universe is not outside (the māyā).
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā differs: You have explained it well that ātman (individual Soul) and māyā (prakṛti) are Hari’s place of residence. The source of the universe (i.e. Brahman) is outside it and has not created it. Therefore the universe being rootless appears purposeless, a delusion.
(ii) Padaratnāvalī explains. Oh possessor of the proper knowledge for realization of Brahman, your explanation that all this is due to Hari’s own power called māyā is convincing enough to clear up all doubts. The iḍea of delusion in the māyā concept is dispelled. But all this appears to me purposeless and baseless except on the presumption of adṛṣṭa.

devanagari: यश्च मूढतमो लोके यश्च बुद्धेः परं गतः । तावुभौ सुखमेधेते क्लिश्यत्यन्तरितो जनः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: yaśca mūḍhatamo loke yaśca buddheḥ paraṃ gataḥ tāvubhau sukhamedhete kliśyatyantarito janaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ kliś class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ kliś class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ kliś class 4 verb], [locative single from √ kliś class 4 verb]

translation: Only those two, viz. one who is the stupid-most (and attached to physical body etc.) and one who has reached beyond Buddhi (i.e. Prakṛti and realized God live happily in the world. But the person who is between these (extremes), undergoes sufferings (as he desires to leave the worldly life but cannot do so, for want of experience of the inner bliss.

devanagari: अर्थाभावं विनिश्चित्य प्रतीतस्यापि नात्मनः । तां चापि युष्मच्चरण सेवयाहं पराणुदे ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: arthābhāvaṃ viniścitya pratītasyāpi nātmanaḥ tāṃ cāpi yuṣmaccaraṇa sevayāhaṃ parāṇude
translation: Having determined the unreality of the world despite its apparent existence, I shall dispel even that sense by serving your feet.

devanagari: यत्सेवया भगवतः कूटस्थस्य मधुद्विषः । रतिरासो भवेत्तीव्रः पादयोर्व्यसनार्दनः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: yatsevayā bhagavataḥ kūṭasthasya madhudviṣaḥ ratirāso bhavettīvraḥ pādayorvyasanārdanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: It is by service (of persons like you) that ardent intensity of love for the feet of the changeless Lord Hari (the enemy of the demon Madhu), is created leading to the destruction of miseries (saṃsāra).

devanagari: दुरापा ह्यल्पतपसः सेवा वैकुण्ठवर्त्मसु । यत्रोपगीयते नित्यं देवदेवो जनार्दनः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: durāpā hyalpatapasaḥ sevā vaikuṇṭhavartmasu yatropagīyate nityaṃ devadevo janārdanaḥ
translation: The service of those who are on the pathway to Vaikuṇṭha,[6] and amongst whom the deeds of Janārdana, the God of gods are always eulogised, is very difficult to be obtained (for rendering) by people (who have little penance to their credit).
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Or: who can guide to the path of Vaikuṇṭha—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: सृष्ट्वाग्रे महदादीनि सविकाराणि अनुक्रमात् । तेभ्यो विराजं उद्धृत्य तमनु प्राविशद्विभुः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: sṛṣṭvāgre mahadādīni savikārāṇi anukramāt tebhyo virājaṃ uddhṛtya tamanu prāviśadvibhuḥ
translation: (You said that) having created in the beginning, the principles like mahat and others with their modifications in due course, and having raised the Virāj (the Egg of the universe) out of them, the All-pervading Lord entered into it.

devanagari: यमाहुराद्यं पुरुषं सहस्राङ्घ्र्यूरुबाहुकम् । यत्र विश्व इमे लोकाः सविकाशं समासते ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: yamāhurādyaṃ puruṣaṃ sahasrāṅghryūrubāhukam yatra viśva ime lokāḥ savikāśaṃ samāsate
translation: (The Virāj) whois called the First Man (ādya Puruṣa) possessing thousands) of feet, thighs and arms, and in whom all the worlds along with their modifications are accommodated.

devanagari: यस्मिन् दशविधः प्राणः सेन्द्रियार्थेन्द्रियः त्रिवृत् । त्वयेरितो यतो वर्णाः तद्विभूतीर्वदस्व नः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: yasmin daśavidhaḥ prāṇaḥ sendriyārthendriyaḥ trivṛt tvayerito yato varṇāḥ tadvibhūtīrvadasva naḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative active second single]
[accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: As explained by you, in him are the vital-airs (prāṇas) of ten kinds, and also in the three forms viz. the sense-organs, the objects and the presiding deities of the sense-organs (also called indriyas—sense-organs—here), and;the castes (varṇas) came forth (were formed) from him. Please describe to us his powers.

devanagari: यत्र पुत्रैश्च पौत्रैश्च नप्तृभिः सह गोत्रजैः । प्रजा विचित्राकृतय आसन्याभिरिदं ततम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: yatra putraiśca pautraiśca naptṛbhiḥ saha gotrajaiḥ prajā vicitrākṛtaya āsanyābhiridaṃ tatam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ tan class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ tan class 8 verb]

translation: (Virāṭ) wherein lived beings of different forms, with their sons, grandsons, daughter’s sons and other descendants of their gotra (clan) who have filled the world.

devanagari: प्रजापतीनां स पतिः चकॢपे कान् प्रजापतीन् । सर्गांश्चैवानुसर्गांश्च मनून् मन्वन्तराधिपान् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: prajāpatīnāṃ sa patiḥ cakḷpe kān prajāpatīn sargāṃścaivānusargāṃśca manūn manvantarādhipān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Who were the lords of the created beings (prajāpatis) created by lord of Prajāpatis (Brahmadeva)? What are (god Brahmā’s) creations, subcreations? Who were the Manus and kings in the manvantaras (periods of Manus) created by him?

devanagari: एतेषामपि वंशांश्च वंशानुचरितानि च । उपर्यधश्च ये लोका भूमेर्मित्रात्मजासते ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: eteṣāmapi vaṃśāṃśca vaṃśānucaritāni ca uparyadhaśca ye lokā bhūmermitrātmajāsate
translation: Oh Maitreya, describe to me their dynasties, and the deeds of those born in their families; and the situation and the area, dimensions of this world and of the worlds that are above and below this.

devanagari: तेषां संस्थां प्रमाणं च भूर्लोकस्य च वर्णय । तिर्यङ्मानुषदेवानां सरीसृप पतत्त्रिणाम् । वद नः सर्गसंव्यूहं गार्भस्वेदद्विजोद्भिदाम् ॥ २७ ...
english_devnagari: teṣāṃ saṃsthāṃ pramāṇaṃ ca bhūrlokasya ca varṇaya tiryaṅmānuṣadevānāṃ sarīsṛpa patattriṇām vada naḥ sargasaṃvyūhaṃ gārbhasvedadvijodbhidām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pat class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pat class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ pat class 1 verb]

translation: Tell us about the creation and classification of beasts, human beings, gods, reptiles and birds and those born of womb, sweat, eggs (or twice-born) and vegetables.

devanagari: गुणावतारैर्विश्वस्य सर्गस्थित्यप्ययाश्रयम् । सृजतः श्रीनिवासस्य व्याचक्ष्वोदारविक्रमम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: guṇāvatārairviśvasya sargasthityapyayāśrayam sṛjataḥ śrīnivāsasya vyācakṣvodāravikramam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [ablative single from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [genitive single from √ sṛj class 6 verb]
[ablative single from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [genitive single from √ sṛj class 6 verb]

translation: Expound to us the great exploits ofViṣṇu, the creator, the support of the (process of) creation, sustenance and destruction of the world through his guṇāvatāras (incarnations characterised by guṇas, viz. sattva, rajas and tamas, such as Brahmā etc.)

devanagari: वर्णाश्रमविभागांश्च रूपशीलस्वभावतः । ऋषीणां जन्मकर्माणि वेदस्य च विकर्षणम् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: varṇāśramavibhāgāṃśca rūpaśīlasvabhāvataḥ ṛṣīṇāṃ janmakarmāṇi vedasya ca vikarṣaṇam
translation: Oh Lord (Maitreya), explain the classification of Varṇas (castes) and stages of life (āśramas) according to their external characteristics, courses of conduct and nature, the births and deeds of sages, the division of the Vedas, the details of sacrifices, the paths of Yoga, Sāṅkhya characterised by renunciation of the fruit of Karmas, and the Tantra as enjoined by the Supreme Lord.

devanagari: यज्ञस्य च वितानानि योगस्य च पथः प्रभो । नैष्कर्म्यस्य च साङ्ख्यस्य तंत्रं वा भगवत्स्मृतम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: yajñasya ca vitānāni yogasya ca pathaḥ prabho naiṣkarmyasya ca sāṅkhyasya taṃtraṃ vā bhagavatsmṛtam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb]

translation: Oh Lord (Maitreya), explain the classification of Varṇas (castes) and stages of life (āśramas) according to their external characteristics, courses of conduct and nature, the births and deeds of sages, the division of the Vedas, the details of sacrifices, the paths of Yoga, Sāṅkhya characterised by renunciation of the fruit of Karmas, and the Tantra as enjoined by the Supreme Lord.

devanagari: पाषण्डपथवैषम्यं प्रतिलोमनिवेशनम् । जीवस्य गतयो याश्च यावतीर्गुणकर्मजाः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: pāṣaṇḍapathavaiṣamyaṃ pratilomaniveśanam jīvasya gatayo yāśca yāvatīrguṇakarmajāḥ
translation:
Oh sinless one, explain to me in details:
The crookedness of the path of the non-believers in Veda, the place etc. of the progeny of the reverse (pratiloma) type of marriages, and the several courses of jīva as a result of their attributes (guṇas) and deeds (karmas).

devanagari: धर्मार्थकाममोक्षाणां निमित्तान्यविरोधतः । वार्ताया दण्डनीतेश्च श्रुतस्य च विधिं पृथक् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: dharmārthakāmamokṣāṇāṃ nimittānyavirodhataḥ vārtāyā daṇḍanīteśca śrutasya ca vidhiṃ pṛthak
translation: The ways of accomplishing the puruṣārthas (objects of life such as dharma, artha, kāma and mokṣa) without mutual conflict, the different methods of commerce and agriculture, the duties of kings (or politics), and the courses of study.

devanagari: श्राद्धस्य च विधिं ब्रह्मन् पितॄणां सर्गमेव च । ग्रहनक्षत्रताराणां कालावयवसंस्थितिम् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrāddhasya ca vidhiṃ brahman pitṝṇāṃ sargameva ca grahanakṣatratārāṇāṃ kālāvayavasaṃsthitim
translation: The prescribed way of performing śrāddha, the creation of the manes (pitṛs) and the arrangement of planets, constellations and stars in the division of Time.

devanagari: दानस्य तपसो वापि यच्चेष्टापूर्तयोः फलम् । प्रवासस्थस्य यो धर्मो यश्च पुंस उतापदि ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: dānasya tapaso vāpi yacceṣṭāpūrtayoḥ phalam pravāsasthasya yo dharmo yaśca puṃsa utāpadi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: The fruits of charitable gifts, of penance, of meritorious acts (such as performance of sacrifices, constructions of amenities like tanks etc.), the religious duties of men on travel, and of men in adverse circumstances.

devanagari: येन वा भगवान् तुस्तुष्येद् धर्मयोनिर्जनार्दनः । सम्प्रसीदति वा येषां एतत् आख्याहि मेऽनघ ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: yena vā bhagavān tustuṣyed dharmayonirjanārdanaḥ samprasīdati vā yeṣāṃ etat ākhyāhi me
translation: The course (of action) whereby Janārdana, the source of Dharma will be propitiated, and favours one with his grace.

devanagari: अनुव्रतानां शिष्याणां पुत्राणां च द्विजोत्तम । अनापृष्टमपि ब्रूयुः गुरवो दीनवत्सलाः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: anuvratānāṃ śiṣyāṇāṃ putrāṇāṃ ca dvijottama anāpṛṣṭamapi brūyuḥ guravo dīnavatsalāḥ
translation: Oh best among the twice-born persons! Teachers who are kindly disposed to the distressed, explain to obedient disciples and sons, even without being enquired about it,

devanagari: तत्त्वानां भगवन् तेषां कतिधा प्रतिसङ्क्रमः । तत्रेमं क उपासीरन् क उ स्विदनुशेरते ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: tattvānāṃ bhagavan teṣāṃ katidhā pratisaṅkramaḥ tatremaṃ ka upāsīran ka u svidanuśerate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Oh revered one! In how many ways is the withdrawal (destruction) of the principles takes effect? At that time who will resort to him and who will follow him in his yogic sleep?

devanagari: पुरुषस्य च संस्थानं स्वरूपं वा परस्य च । ज्ञानं च नैगमं यत्तद् गुरुशिष्यप्रयोजनम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: puruṣasya ca saṃsthānaṃ svarūpaṃ vā parasya ca jñānaṃ ca naigamaṃ yattad guruśiṣyaprayojanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: (Tell us) what the real nature of jīva is, and the essential state of the Supreme Soul and the knowledge pertaining to Upaniṣads, and the purpose of the pupil-teacher relationship.

devanagari: निमित्तानि च तस्येह प्रोक्तान्यनघसूरिभिः । स्वतो ज्ञानं कुतः पुंसां भक्तिर्वैराग्यमेव वा ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: nimittāni ca tasyeha proktānyanaghasūribhiḥ svato jñānaṃ kutaḥ puṃsāṃ bhaktirvairāgyameva vā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [present passive first single], [imperative active second single]

translation: Oh sinless Maitreya, explain the means prescribed by the wise ones, for the acquisition of that knowledge here. Otherwise how can that knowledge, devotion or non-attachment develop in men automatically (if there be no grace of the spiritual guide)?

devanagari: एतान्मे पृच्छतः प्रश्नान् हरेः कर्मविवित्सया । ब्रूहि मेऽज्ञस्य मित्रत्वात् अजया नष्टचक्षुषः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: etānme pṛcchataḥ praśnān hareḥ karmavivitsayā brūhi me

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ praś class 6 verb], [ablative single from √ praś class 6 verb], [genitive single from √ praś class 6 verb]
[ablative single from √ praś class 6 verb], [genitive single from √ praś class 6 verb]
[vocative single from √ naś class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ naś class 4 verb]
[vocative single from √ naś class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ naś class 4 verb]

translation: As a friend, please tell me who am making these queries with a desire to know the deeds of Hari, and who am ignorant due to the loss of the vision of knowledge through (the influence of) māyā.

devanagari: सर्वे वेदाश्च यज्ञाश्च तपो दानानि चानघ । जीवाभयप्रदानस्य न कुर्वीरन् कलामपि ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: sarve vedāśca yajñāśca tapo dānāni cānagha jīvābhayapradānasya na kurvīran kalāmapi
translation: Oh sinless, one all Vedas, sacrifices, penances and charitable donations will not amount even to a fraction of the act of offering shelter to a Soul (by imparting the knowledge of the Truth).

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । स इत्थं आपृष्टपुराणकल्पः । कुरुप्रधानेन मुनिप्रधानः । प्रवृद्धहर्षो भगवत्कथायां । सञ्चोदितस्तं प्रहसन्निवाह ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca sa itthaṃ āpṛṣṭapurāṇakalpaḥ kurupradhānena munipradhānaḥ pravṛddhaharṣo bhagavatkathāyāṃ sañcoditastaṃ prahasannivāha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
The prominent sage who was thus asked by a prominent person among the Kurus, about matters explained in the Purāṇas was overjoyed at being urged to narrate the episodes of the Lord, and spoke to him smilingly.
